title: Ginger People audit — strong proof, leaking buy path and mobile speed
executive_summary: **Ginger People has the demand and the proof; the buy path is leaking it.** The storefront leads with strong proof — "World's #1 selling ginger candy," "10x more ginger," a pregnancy testimonial — over a 44-product catalog spanning candy, juice, and gingerade. But `/cart` returns a branded 404, and the homepage mixes internal "SHOP NOW" links with a raw Amazon handoff, so high-intent sessions hit dead ends and inconsistent buy paths.

**A large, use-case-rich catalog is presented as a flat list.** The 44 SKUs map cleanly to missions — nausea and travel, cooking, candy, drinks — but the products area asks shoppers to decode product names instead of shopping their job-to-be-done, and consumables like ginger juice and gingerade sell one-time only despite being natural repeat purchases.

**Mobile speed is the silent tax on all of it.** PageSpeed mobile scored 23 on a heavy homepage, throttling every session before the proof can do its work. The first test should be structural: fix the `/cart` dead end and unify the buying handoff, then organize the catalog by need and recover mobile performance.

experiments.title: Make the empty cart useful
experiments.hypothesis: High-intent returning sessions are recovered by replacing the /cart 404 with a purchase-recovery page. The captured /cart returns a branded 404, and for a retailer-routed brand /cart is a common destination for returning users, old links, browser memory, and tracking tools — a dead end there silently loses demand.
experiments.primary_change: Replace the /cart 404 with a branded recovery page offering "Continue shopping," "Shop online retailers," "Find a store," and support links.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if /cart exit rate drops without hurting site-wide conversion.

experiments.title: Unify the buying handoff
experiments.hypothesis: Outbound-purchase clarity improves CVR by giving every hero and product hub one consistent "Buy online / Find near me" module. The captured homepage routes one "SHOP NOW" to a raw Amazon URL and others to internal hubs, so the purchase action is ambiguous and inconsistent across the page.
experiments.primary_change: Add a consistent buying box (retailer logos + "Buy online" + "Find near me") to the hero and each product hub, replacing the ad-hoc mix of internal and Amazon links.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if outbound retailer click-through improves without hurting homepage bounce rate.

experiments.title: Make the Products page need-first
experiments.hypothesis: CVR improves by presenting the catalog by shopper mission instead of a flat product list. The captured catalog spans 44 SKUs with distinct use cases (nausea/travel, cooking, candy, drinks), but the products area asks shoppers to decode product names rather than shop their job-to-be-done.
experiments.primary_change: Restructure /products/ into need-first blocks ("For nausea + travel," "For cooking," "For candy lovers," "For drinks") each with a use-case header and a "find your match" CTA.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if products-page click-through improves without hurting PDP-to-purchase conversion.

experiments.title: Build a GIN GINS flavor sampler
experiments.affected_surface: GIN GINS category + new sampler PDP
experiments.hypothesis: AOV rises when first-time candy shoppers can buy a multi-flavor trial instead of committing to one flavor. The captured catalog lists 6+ GIN GINS flavors (mandarin, lemon, spicy apple, peanut, spicy turmeric, super strength) with no sampler entry point.
experiments.primary_change: Launch a "GIN GINS Flavor Tour" sampler bundling the core flavors, promoted on the GIN GINS category.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if first-time AOV rises by ≥$4 without hurting overall candy conversion.

experiments.title: Sell a travel nausea kit
experiments.hypothesis: AOV rises by bundling the nausea-relief formats into one travel-ready kit. The captured homepage positions Ginger Rescue for "nausea, motion sickness & indigestion" — one of the highest-intent use cases — but sells the formats separately with no kit.
experiments.primary_change: Launch a "Travel Stomach Rescue Kit" (lozenges + chewables) positioned for motion sickness, morning sickness, and travel, promoted on Ginger Rescue PDPs.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if AOV rises by ≥$3 without hurting Ginger Rescue conversion.

experiments.title: Package a daily ginger + turmeric routine
experiments.hypothesis: Repeat purchase rate improves by turning a daily wellness habit into a named routine shoppers can reorder. The captured homepage promotes a ginger + turmeric gift set and Turmeric Gingerade but offers no reorderable daily routine.
experiments.primary_change: Create a "Daily Ginger + Turmeric Routine" bundle (gingerade + turmeric formats) with a subscribe option, placed on the gingerade PDPs and a routine hub.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if 30-day repeat purchase rate improves without hurting first-order AOV.

experiments.title: Add subscribe-and-save to consumable juices
experiments.hypothesis: Reorder rate improves by offering subscribe-and-save on consumables. The captured catalog includes ginger juice and gingerade — regular repeat purchases — sold one-time only, so loyal customers must reorder manually each time.
experiments.primary_change: Add a "Subscribe & save" option to consumable juice and gingerade PDPs with an editable cadence.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if 90-day repeat purchase rate improves without hurting first-order conversion.

experiments.title: Create a pregnancy nausea page
experiments.hypothesis: Landing-page CVR improves by matching high-anxiety morning-sickness visitors to testimonials and product choices in one compliant destination. The captured homepage carries a strong pregnancy testimonial ("I'm pregnant... 34 weeks... settled my tummy"), but morning-sickness intent has no dedicated page to land on.
experiments.primary_change: Create /morning-sickness-ginger/ using existing testimonials and Ginger Rescue / GIN GINS recommendations, with "ask your clinician" language and ingredient transparency.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if landing-page CVR exceeds existing health pages without compliance flags.

experiments.title: Help shoppers find their ginger
experiments.hypothesis: Landing-page CVR improves by routing health-intent and snack-intent visitors to the right product family via a short quiz. The captured homepage uses need language (nausea, motion sickness, digestion) but drops shoppers straight into product hubs with no guided path.
experiments.primary_change: Create /find-your-ginger/ with prompts for nausea, travel, cooking, daily wellness, and candy, each returning a recommendation and a "buy online / find near me" choice.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if landing-page CVR improves without hurting site-wide conversion rate.

experiments.title: Recover mobile page speed
experiments.hypothesis: Bounce drops as mobile largest-contentful-paint improves. PageSpeed mobile scored 23 on a heavy WordPress homepage with large hero images and many third-party scripts, so most sessions are throttled before the proof can land.
experiments.primary_change: Compress and serve responsive images, lazy-load below-fold media, defer non-critical scripts, and trim unused plugins.
experiments.decision_rule: Ship if mobile page speed improves without hurting conversion.

competitor_intro: Competitors make the shopping job easier through use-case clarity, symptom-led navigation, retailer handoffs, and flavor-led merchandising. Ginger People's edge is deeper ginger specialization, proof, reviews, recipes, health education, and broader formats.

competitors.what_they_make_easier: Immediate use-case clarity for nausea and travel.
competitors.store_edge: Natural ginger formats and candy/snack permission.
competitors.pattern_to_adapt: Dedicated nausea/travel pages with product-choice modules.

competitors.positioning: Ginger/peppermint drops for nausea and digestive comfort
competitors.store_edge: Broader catalog, recipes, and mainstream candy formats.
Deeper ginger specialization and health education.
competitors.pattern_to_adapt: Stronger retailer handoff per product family.

competitors.store_edge: Stronger functional use cases, reviews, recipes, and family story.

tech_checks.detail: Title, meta description, and Open Graph tags present.

tech_checks.name: Structured Data
tech_checks.detail: JSON-LD structured data found on the homepage.

tech_checks.name: Image Optimization
tech_checks.detail: Byte-level image weight not measured in this pass.

tech_checks.name: Cookie/Privacy
tech_checks.detail: Privacy policy link present; consent mechanics not inspected.
